zh.Zevenbergen, North Brabant, Netherlands: 荷蘭努-布拉班特斯文爾根
zh.lifeSummary: 當Elisabeth van Strijen於1262年出生在荷蘭-拉藍德斯特利恩時，她的父親Willem II van Strijen48歲，母親Elisabeth van Arkel43歲。她於1280年與Gerard III van Egmond在荷蘭努-拉藍德艾姆-賓結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1297年12月16日在荷蘭努-拉藍德艾姆德享年35歲，埋葬在荷蘭努-拉藍德艾姆德。

zh-hans.Zevenbergen, North Brabant, Netherlands: 荷兰努-布拉班特斯文爾根
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elisabeth van Strijen于1262年出生在荷兰-拉藍德斯特利恩时，她的父亲Willem II van Strijen48岁，母亲Elisabeth van Arkel43岁。她于1280年与Gerard III van Egmond在荷兰努-拉藍德艾姆-賓结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1297年12月16日在荷兰努-拉藍德艾姆德享年35岁，埋葬在荷兰努-拉藍德艾姆德。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Elisabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

variants.name: Elizabeth
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Elisabeth van Strijen was born in 1262, in Strijen, South Holland, Netherlands, her father, Willem II van Strijen, was 48 and her mother, Elisabeth van Arkel, was 43. She married Gerard III van Egmond in 1280, in Egmond-Binnen, North Holland, Netherlands. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 16 December 1297, in Egmond, North Holland, Netherlands, at the age of 35, and was buried in Egmond, North Holland, Netherlands.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elisabeth van Strijen est née en 1262, à Strijen, Hollande-Méridionale, Pays-Bas, son père, Willem II van Strijen, avait 48 ans et sa mère, Elisabeth van Arkel, avait 43 ans. Elle a épousé Gerard III van Egmond en 1280, à Egmond-Binnen, Hollande-septentrionale, Pays-Bas. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 16 décembre 1297, à Egmond, Hollande-septentrionale, Pays-Bas, à l’âge de 35 ans, et a été enterrée à Egmond, Hollande-septentrionale, Pays-Bas.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elisabeth van Strijen 1262 in Strijen, Südholland, Niederlande geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Willem II van Strijen 48 und ihre Mutter Elisabeth van Arkel 43. Sie hat 1280 in Egmond-Binnen, Nord-Holland, Niederlande Gerard III van Egmond geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 16. Dezember 1297 im Alter von 35 Jahren in Egmond, Nord-Holland, Niederlande und wurde in Egmond, Nord-Holland, Niederlande begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elisabeth van Strijen è nata il nel 1262 nel Strijen, Zuid-Holland, Paesi Bassi, sua padre, Willem II van Strijen, aveva 48 anni e sua madre, Elisabeth van Arkel, aveva 43 anni. Lei sposato Gerard III van Egmond nel 1280, nel Egmond-Binnen, Noord-Holland, Paesi Bassi. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 16 dicembre 1297, nel Egmond, Noord-Holland, Paesi Bassi, all'età di 35 anni, e fu sepolta a Egmond, Noord-Holland, Paesi Bassi.

ja.lifeSummary: Elisabeth van Strijenが1262年にオランダズイド-hオランドストリジェンで生まれた時，父Willem II van Strijenは48歳，母Elisabeth van Arkelは43歳であった。1280年にオランダノオルド-hオランドエグモンド-ビンでGerard III van Egmondと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1297年12月16日にオランダノオルド-hオランドエグモンドで35歳で死去し，オランダノオルド-hオランドエグモンドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elisabeth van Strijen(이)가 1262년 네덜란드 주이드-홀랜드 스트리젠에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Willem II van Strijen(은)는 48세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elisabeth van Arkel(은)는 43세였다. 그녀는 1280년 네덜란드 노르드-홀랜드 그몽-빈에서 Gerard III van Egmond(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1297년 12월 16일 네덜란드 노르드-홀랜드 그몽에서 35세의 나이로 사망했으며 네덜란드 노르드-홀랜드 그몽에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elisabeth van Strijen nasceu em 1262, em Strijen, Zuid-Holland, Países Baixos, seu pai, Willem II van Strijen, tinha 48 anos e sua mãe, Elisabeth van Arkel, tinha 43 anos. Ela casou-se com Gerard III van Egmond em 1280, em Egmond-Binnen, Holanda do Norte, Países Baixos. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 16 de dezembro de 1297, em Egmond, Holanda do Norte, Países Baixos, com 35 anos, e foi sepultada em Egmond, Holanda do Norte, Países Baixos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elisabeth van Strijen родилась в 1262 г. в местности стриджен, зуид-холланд, Нидерланды, её отцу по имени Willem II van Strijen было 48 лет, а матери, которую звали Elisabeth van Arkel, 43. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Gerard III van Egmond в 1280 г. в местности эгмонд-биннен, нурд-холланд, Нидерланды. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла 16 декабря 1297 г. в местности эгмонд, нурд-холланд, Нидерланды в возрасте 35 лет и была похоронена в местности эгмонд, нурд-холланд, Нидерланды.

es.Zevenbergen, North Brabant, Netherlands: Zevenbergen, Brabante del Norte, Países Bajos
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elisabeth van Strijen nació en 1262, en Strijen, Zuid-Holland, Países Bajos, su papá, Willem II van Strijen, tenía 48 años y su mamá, Elisabeth van Arkel, tenía 43 años. Se casó con Gerard III van Egmond en 1280, en Egmond-Binnen, Noord-Holland, Países Bajos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 16 de diciembre de 1297, en Egmond, Noord-Holland, Países Bajos, a la edad de 35 años, y fue sepultada en Egmond, Noord-Holland, Países Bajos.
